title: These 3 Homebuilders Fit the Value Stock Profile
text: Each one of these three residential construction stocks fit the traditional value stock profile: low price-earnings ratios, not too far from book value, decent earnings records, low debt profiles and paying dividends. These are the sorts of valuation metrics Benjamin Graham wrote about in his classic works on the subject.

title: Homebuilding Market Flourishing Despite All Odds: 4 Winners
text: The homebuilding market is on solid gound, which is helping stocks like D.R. Horton (DHI), Toll Brothers (TOL), KB Home (KBH) and Lennar Corporation (LEN).

title: 7 Discounted Dividend Stocks With Market-Beating Yields
text: While there is ample supply of dividend stocks to be found on Wall Street, finding high-quality ones whose payouts are secure can be a little more tricky.  What's more, following years of big gains in the equity markets, stock prices for many of the best dividend payers is in the clouds.

title: Homebuilding Continues to Drive Construction Spending: 4 Picks
text: Spending on residential construction projects have been helping the construction sector, which is likely to bode well for stocks like Toll Brothers (TOL), KB Home (KBH), PulteGroup (PHM) and Lennar Corporation (LEN).

title: Housing Goes From Graham to Munger
text: Ben Graham is ascribed as being the father of value investing. The intellectual framework he brought to investors was using the available accounting to measure value of a business.

title: New Strong Buy Stocks for February 1st
text: LEN, HZO, NUE, AB, and RHI have been added to the Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) List on February 1, 2022.
